content:
{
  "market_assessment": {
    "bias": "CAUTIOUS",
    "reasoning": "Nifty -1.14%, Bank Nifty -1.29%, VIX +9.8% to 18.49 — clear risk-off tape with Realty/PSU Banks/Auto leading down. However, Pharma (+0.71%) and IT (+0.29%) showing defensive rotation, and FMCG names ripping (TATACONSUM +6%, MARICO at ATH). My existing book is well-positioned for this rotation (heavy FMCG/Pharma/Chemicals), so focus is on protecting winners via trailing SLs, not chasing extended breakouts.",
    "key_levels": {
      "nifty_support": 23800,
      "nifty_resistance": 24100
    }
  },
  "decisions": [
    {
      "action": "HOLD",
      "symbol": "GOLDBEES",
      "exchange": "NSE",
      "product": "CNC",
      "confidence": 0.6,
      "timeframe": "SWING",
      "reasoning": "Gold ETF -1.1%, mild intraday weakness on profit-taking. Safe-haven thesis intact with VIX +9.8% and geopolitical premium. SL 121 is ~2.2% below CMP — adequate buffer. No trail yet."
    },
    {
      "action": "MODIFY",
      "symbol": "MARICO",
      "exchange": "NSE",
      "product": "CNC",
      "new_stop_loss": 828.00,
      "new_target": 860.00,
      "confidence": 0.7,
      "timeframe": "SWING",
      "reasoning": "MARICO at 52-week high 843, +2.5% unrealized. SL already at 828 (above entry 820 = breakeven+). Q4 PAT +14%, FMCG defensive rotation intact, MOFSL +15% upside. Holding SL at 828 — already locks gains, gap to CMP 839 is ~1.4% which respects the 1% trail-gap floor. Nudging target to 860 to capture sector momentum."
    },
    {
      "action": "MODIFY",
      "symbol": "COFORGE",
      "exchange": "NSE",
      "product": "CNC",
      "new_stop_loss": 1357.20,
      "new_target": 1450.00,
      "confidence": 0.75,
      "timeframe": "SWING",
      "reasoning": "COFORGE +9.9% unrealized, today +7.75% on Q4 beat + Cigniti merger record date May 16. SL 1357.20 currently ~2% below CMP 1384.90 — locked in ~+7.8% from entry 1259. Trail-gap to CMP is 2%, respects 1% floor. Bullish print + brokerage PTs up to 2020 justify lifting target to 1450 (R:R math at entry: risk 0, all locked profit). HOLD existing SL — within 1% gap rule and Cigniti supply on May 16 is a near-term overhang argues against tightening further into event."
    },
    {
      "action": "HOLD",
      "symbol": "PIDILITIND",
      "exchange": "NSE",
      "product": "CNC",
      "confidence": 0.55,
      "timeframe": "SWING",
      "reasoning": "PIDILITIND -3.1% unrealized, CMP 1443.80 vs SL 1465. Per Rule 17 — do NOT front-run SL. CMP is BELOW stored SL 1465 actually... wait: SL 1465 > CMP 1443 means SL would have already triggered. Checking: stored SL is 1465 (above CMP). This is concerning — but data shows position still held, so SL may be pending broker execution. Q4 PAT +37%, UVG 15.3% — thesis intact. Letting SL do its job; if not already triggered, it will fire on next downtick. Maintaining stored SL."
    },
    {
      "action": "MODIFY",
      "symbol": "WOCKPHARMA",
      "exchange": "NSE",
      "product": "CNC",
      "new_stop_loss": 1635.00,
      "new_target": 1750.00,
      "confidence": 0.75,
      "timeframe": "SWING",
      "reasoning": "WOCKPHARMA +2.6%, CMP 1656 vs entry 1618. Q4 EBITDA +148% YoY, Pharma at 52w highs, Zaynich EMA pathway. Raising SL from 1618 (entry) to 1635 — still 1.3% below CMP (respects 1% trail-gap floor) and locks ~+1% gain. Raising target to 1750 toward 52w high zone."
    },
    {
      "action": "HOLD",
      "symbol": "SRF",
      "exchange": "NSE",
      "product": "CNC",
      "confidence": 0.65,
      "timeframe": "SWING",
      "reasoning": "SRF +0.4%, CMP 2790 vs SL 2760 (1.1% below CMP). Q4 PAT +10.6%, FY26 PAT +46.7%, Odisha refrigerant capex bullish. Not yet at +2% to justify trail to BE. Letting setup develop."
    },
    {
      "action": "HOLD",
      "symbol": "POLYCAB",
      "exchange": "NSE",
      "product": "CNC",
      "confidence": 0.6,
      "timeframe": "SWING",
      "reasoning": "POLYCAB -0.2%, CMP 9076 near 52w high 9050. Q4 PAT +7%, brokerage PTs up to 10500, 30% W&C market share. SL 8950 is 1.4% below CMP, adequate. Not stretched yet — hold."
    },
    {
      "action": "HOLD",
      "symbol": "BAJAJ-AUTO",
      "exchange": "NSE",
      "product": "CNC",
      "confidence": 0.6,
      "timeframe": "SWING",
      "reasoning": "BAJAJ-AUTO -1.6%, CMP 10556 vs SL 10440 (1.1% below CMP). Q4 standalone PAT +34%, buyback at 12000 = price floor near-term, dividend+buyback record May 29. Auto sector weak today -1.65% but BAJAJ-AUTO holding up on buyback support. SL respected — hold."
    },
    {
      "action": "HOLD",
      "symbol": "DABUR",
      "exchange": "NSE",
      "product": "CNC",
      "confidence": 0.55,
      "timeframe": "SWING",
      "reasoning": "DABUR -1.8%, CMP 476.95 vs SL 468 (1.9% below CMP). Q4 PAT +15.75%, quick commerce +54%, FMCG defensive rotation. SL not at risk yet — hold, let thesis play out."
    },
    {
      "action": "MODIFY",
      "symbol": "LAURUSLABS",
      "exchange": "NSE",
      "product": "CNC",
      "new_stop_loss": 1245.00,
      "new_target": 1320.00,
      "confidence": 0.75,
      "timeframe": "SWING",
      "reasoning": "LAURUSLABS +3.6%, at all-time high 1267 vs entry 1226. Q4 PAT +19%, FDA audit cleared, CDMO ramp, Pharma at 52w highs. Currently SL 1240 is BELOW entry — that's a no-op state. Raising SL to 1245 (above entry, locks +1.5%) and respects 1% gap rule (CMP 1265.80 - SL 1245 = 1.6% gap). Raising target to 1320 — clear ATH breakout territory."
    },
    {
      "action": "HOLD",
      "symbol": "TATACONSUM",
      "exchange": "NSE",
      "product": "CNC",
      "confidence": 0.6,
      "timeframe": "SWING",
      "reasoning": "TATACONSUM -2.5%, CMP 1221.80 — but day high was 1253 and it's gapping up post Q4 (+6% intraday from prior close 1151). Entry 1248 was at the high; fading off intraday peak is normal. Q4 PAT +21.5%, India UVG 16% sector-best. SL 1198 is 1.9% below CMP — adequate. Rule 17 — do not front-run SL; thesis intact, hold."
    },
    {
      "action": "HOLD",
      "symbol": "CGPOWER",
      "exchange": "NSE",
      "product": "CNC",
      "confidence": 0.65,
      "timeframe": "SWING",
      "reasoning": "CGPOWER +0.3%, CMP 864.55 near 52w high 872. Q4 PAT +32%, order book +61% YoY, power segment +50%. SL 838 is 3% below CMP — gap respected, not yet at +2% to trail to BE. Hold."
    }
  ],
  "position_actions": [
    {"symbol": "GOLDBEES", "current_action": "HOLD", "new_stop_loss": null, "reasoning": "Safe-haven intact, SL 121 has 2.2% buffer."},
    {"symbol": "MARICO", "current_action": "TRAIL_SL", "new_stop_loss": 828.00, "reasoning": "Already trailed to BE+, target nudged up to 860."},
    {"symbol": "COFORGE", "current_action": "HOLD", "new_stop_loss": 1357.20, "reasoning": "SL already locks +7.8%; lifting target to 1450 to ride Cigniti merger momentum."},
    {"symbol": "PIDILITIND", "current_action": "HOLD", "new_stop_loss": null, "reasoning": "Thesis intact post Q4 +37%, let stored SL do its job."},
    {"symbol": "WOCKPHARMA", "current_action": "TRAIL_SL", "new_stop_loss": 1635.00, "reasoning": "Locks small gain above entry, respects 1% trail-gap floor."},
    {"symbol": "SRF", "current_action": "HOLD", "new_stop_loss": null, "reasoning": "Not yet at +2% for BE trail; chemicals thesis fresh."},
    {"symbol": "POLYCAB", "current_action": "HOLD", "new_stop_loss": null, "reasoning": "At 52w high, SL 8950 gives adequate room."},
    {"symbol": "BAJAJ-AUTO", "current_action": "HOLD", "new_stop_loss": null, "reasoning": "Buyback at 12000 + dividend creates near-term floor; SL 10440 respected."},
    {"symbol": "DABUR", "current_action": "HOLD", "new_stop_loss": null, "reasoning": "FMCG defensive thesis intact, SL has 1.9% buffer."},
    {"symbol": "LAURUSLABS", "current_action": "TRAIL_SL", "new_stop_loss": 1245.00, "reasoning": "Lifting SL above entry to lock +1.5%; target raised to 1320 on ATH breakout."},
    {"symbol": "TATACONSUM", "current_action": "HOLD", "new_stop_loss": null, "reasoning": "Q4 print bullish, fading off intraday high is normal post gap-up; SL 1198 has 1.9% buffer."},
    {"symbol": "CGPOWER", "current_action": "HOLD", "new_stop_loss": null, "reasoning": "Near 52w high, thesis intact, SL gives 3% buffer."}
  ],
  "watchlist_notes": "Multiple BULLISH watchlist candidates (LAURUSLABS already held, TATACONSUM already held, COFORGE already held, MARICO already held, POLYCAB already held, BAJAJ-AUTO already held, CGPOWER already held, SRF already held, WOCKPHARMA already held, PIDILITIND already held, DABUR already held). New BULLISH names not in book: TORNTPHARM (REJECT — Q4 board May 22 is >3 sessions but stock is at 52w high after a 7-session rally, P/E 62-65x, profit-booking risk extreme, also Rule 11 binary tail in ~7 sessions). PERSISTENT (REJECT — IT sector -25% YTD, MACD bearish crossover, sub-EMA9 setup not clean, stock down ~5% off Q4 highs; would rather add to COFORGE exposure already held). LALPATHLAB (REJECT — neutral sentiment, YoY PAT -15%, already at +8% today on 0.8x volume — chasing a stretched diagnostics name on weak data fails the R:R bar). Deployment at 50% (cash 50%); soft 80% target leaves room, but risk-off tape with VIX +9.8% and Nifty -1.14% means raising deployment via stretched-extension breakouts is the wrong-direction trade. Existing book is already 7 names tilted defensive (Pharma/FMCG/Chemicals) capturing the exact rotation playing out today. Per Rule 18: each qualifying BULLISH candidate has an explicit per-name reject reason above. Will reassess admit candidates on a less volatile tape or on a pullback in named candidates.",
  "portfolio_notes": "Day-22 risk-off tape (VIX +9.8%, Nifty -1.14%) is exactly the environment my Pharma/FMCG/Chemicals-tilted book was built for — TATACONSUM/MARICO/COFORGE/WOCKPHARMA/LAURUSLABS all printing strong Q4s into the rotation. Action this tick is defensive: trail SLs on confirmed winners (MARICO, COFORGE, WOCKPHARMA, LAURUSLABS) without violating the 1% trail-gap floor (Rule 15); HOLD on positions still developing. No new BUYs — 50% deployed is below soft target but tape conviction is low and existing book already expresses the prevailing themes. Cancelling stale HFCL OPEN order should be considered but no MIS-side data provided to confirm it's still relevant — leaving for next tick. Cumulative experiment P&L +0.49% with today +0.67% on track."
}
